---
title: "hsir: Hyper Spectral Imaging R Toolkit"
output: 
  github_document:
    html_preview: false
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{hsir: Hyper Spectral Imaging R Toolkit}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
--- 

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  echo = TRUE
)
```

# hsir <img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="139"/>

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/JannikVindeloev/hsir/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/JannikVindeloev/hsir/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![Codecov test coverage](https://github.com/JannikVindeloev/hsir/actions/workflows/test-coverage.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/JannikVindeloev/hsir/actions/workflows/test-coverage.yaml)
[![Lifecycle: experimental](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-experimental-orange.svg)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html#experimental)
[![License: proprietary](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-proprietary-blue.svg)](https://github.com/JannikVindeloev/hsir/blob/main/LICENSE)
<!-- badges: end -->

## Hyper Spectral Imaging R Toolkit

**hsir** (Hyper Spectral Imaging R Toolkit) provides a lightweight, fast and memory-efficient R toolkit for hyperspectral imaging data analysis and processing.

## Installation

You can install the development version of hsir from GitHub:

```r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("JannikVindeloev/hsir")
```

## Usage

```{r, example}
library(hsir)

# Check if all required packages are available
check_packages()

# Create an optimized data structure for image data
img_data <- matrix(1:100, ncol = 10)
img_dt <- create_image_dt(img_data)
head(img_dt)

# Perform fast matrix operations
mat <- matrix(1:100, ncol = 10)
fast_matrix_ops(mat, "mean")
```

## Performance

The package is designed for high performance:

- Uses `data.table` for fast data manipulation
- Leverages `matrixStats` for optimized matrix operations
- Minimizes memory allocation and copying
- Follows best practices for R package performance

## License

This package is licensed under a proprietary license. See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.
